name: Kewan
bio:
Physical Appearance: Redguard male. Wears a monk robe - simple religious attire appropriate for a devoted worshipper. Armed with an iron dagger.
Race: Redguard
Personality Keywords: devoted, imprisoned, elusive, dedicated
Role: Kewan is a nightblade and worshipper of the Daedric Prince Peryite. He serves at Peryite's Shrine alongside other devotees, but became trapped when a ritual to summon their lord misfired, freezing all the worshippers in place and sending their souls to the Realm of Peryite.
Location: The Daedric Shrine of Peryite. Before being freed, his soul can be found wandering near the huge bonfire in the northeastern area of Peryite's Realm.
The Failed Ritual: Kewan and his fellow worshippers attempted a ritual to summon Peryite. The ritual misfired, freezing their bodies in place at the shrine while trapping their souls in Peryite's Realm. His frozen body remains at the shrine while his soul wanders the Daedric realm.
In Peryite's Realm: Kewan's soul wanders near the large bonfire in the northeastern section of the realm. When approached, he disappears, unable or unwilling to communicate while trapped.
Daily Routine - After Being Freed: Once released from his imprisonment, Kewan returns to his devotions at the shrine with unwavering dedication. He spends 24 hours a day in worship, never eating or sleeping. He divides his time between preaching from behind the lectern, placing offerings to Peryite, sitting on the benches, and wandering around the shrine grounds.
Equipment: Kewan carries an iron dagger for protection and a few coins. He relies heavily on magic, knowing standard nightblade spells along with additional major alteration and minor destruction spells.
Personality - The Tireless Devotee: Kewan's dedication to Peryite is absolute, consuming every hour of his existence. Even after being freed from the traumatic experience of having his soul trapped in a Daedric realm due to a misfired ritual, he immediately returns to his worship without pause, never stopping to eat or sleep. His soul's behavior in Peryite's Realm - wandering near the bonfire but disappearing when approached - suggests either an inability to communicate while trapped or a desire to avoid contact in his imprisoned state. Once freed, he resumes all his old routines in honor of his lord without hesitation or apparent trauma from the ordeal, showing either remarkable faith or perhaps that the experience only deepened his devotion.
author: Bethesda
